search_summary.note: Research covers both original pyfolio and pyfolio-reloaded fork

notes: Bayesian tear sheets require PyMC3 and Theano
Some dependencies may have compatibility issues with latest Python versions in original pyfolio

core_modules.name: pyfolio.tears
core_modules.description: Tear sheet generation - comprehensive visual reports

core_modules.name: pyfolio.timeseries
core_modules.description: Time series analysis and performance metrics

module_inventory.note_on_bayesian: Bayesian module may be optional or integrated differently. create_bayesian_tear_sheet() exists but bayesian.py file not found in master branch.

functions.name: create_full_tear_sheet
functions.signature: create_full_tear_sheet(returns, positions=None, transactions=None, market_data=None, benchmark_rets=None, slippage=None, live_start_date=None, sector_mappings=None, round_trips=False, estimate_intraday='infer', hide_positions=False, cone_std=(1.0, 1.5, 2.0), bootstrap=False, unadjusted_returns=None, turnover_denom='AGB', set_context=True, factor_returns=None, factor_loadings=None, pos_in_dollars=True, header_rows=None, factor_partitions={})
functions.description: Generates comprehensive tear sheets analyzing strategy performance, positions, transactions, and significant events. This is the main entry point for complete portfolio analysis.

parameters.returns: pd.Series - Daily returns of the strategy
parameters.transactions: pd.DataFrame (optional) - Prices and amounts of trades
parameters.round_trips: bool - Include round trip analysis (default: False)
parameters.cone_std: tuple - Standard deviation levels for cone plot (default: (1.0, 1.5, 2.0))
parameters.pos_in_dollars: bool - Positions in dollars vs shares (default: True)

functions.name: create_returns_tear_sheet
functions.signature: create_returns_tear_sheet(returns, positions=None, transactions=None, live_start_date=None, cone_std=(1.0, 1.5, 2.0), benchmark_rets=None, bootstrap=False, turnover_denom='AGB', header_rows=None, return_fig=False)
functions.description: Generates comprehensive returns analysis with rolling metrics, drawdowns, and monthly breakdowns

functions.name: create_position_tear_sheet
functions.signature: create_position_tear_sheet(returns, positions, show_and_plot_top_pos=2, hide_positions=False, sector_mappings=None, transactions=None, estimate_intraday='infer', return_fig=False)
functions.description: Analyzes portfolio positions, holdings, leverage, and exposures

parameters.show_and_plot_top_pos: int - Number of top positions to show (default: 2)

functions.name: create_txn_tear_sheet
functions.signature: create_txn_tear_sheet(returns, positions, transactions, turnover_denom='AGB', unadjusted_returns=None, estimate_intraday='infer', return_fig=False)
functions.description: Analyzes transaction costs, turnover, and daily volume patterns

functions.name: create_round_trip_tear_sheet
functions.signature: create_round_trip_tear_sheet(returns, positions, transactions, sector_mappings=None, estimate_intraday='infer', return_fig=False)
functions.description: Analyzes trade round trips - duration, frequency, and profitability from entry to exit
functions.note: Round trip = opening a position and returning to zero shares

functions.name: create_capacity_tear_sheet
functions.signature: create_capacity_tear_sheet(returns, positions, transactions, market_data, liquidation_daily_vol_limit=0.2, trade_daily_vol_limit=0.05, last_n_days=126, days_to_liquidate_limit=1, estimate_intraday='infer', return_fig=False)
functions.description: Generates portfolio capacity analysis with 'capacity sweep' showing projected Sharpe ratios at various capital bases

parameters.liquidation_daily_vol_limit: float - Max daily volume fraction for liquidation (default: 0.2)
parameters.trade_daily_vol_limit: float - Max daily volume fraction for trades (default: 0.05)

functions.name: create_perf_attrib_tear_sheet
functions.signature: create_perf_attrib_tear_sheet(returns, positions, factor_returns, factor_loadings, transactions=None, pos_in_dollars=True, factor_partitions={}, return_fig=False)
functions.description: Generates performance attribution analysis across risk factors and sectors

functions.name: create_interesting_times_tear_sheet
functions.description: Plots strategy returns during historical market events (dotcom bubble, financial crisis, flash crash, etc.)

parameters.periods: dict (optional) - Custom periods, defaults to interesting_periods.PERIODS

functions.description: Simplified version generating key statistics and plots without market data or sector analysis

interesting_periods_module.description: Historical market event definitions (data module, no functions)

data_structures.name: PERIODS
data_structures.description: Mapping of event/period names to tuples of pandas Timestamp objects representing date ranges for significant market events (dotcom bubble, financial crisis, flash crash, recovery periods, etc.)

tear_sheets.description: Comprehensive visual reports combining multiple plots and statistics
types: Full tear sheet - Complete analysis with all components
Returns tear sheet - Focus on return metrics and distributions
Position tear sheet - Portfolio holdings and exposures
Transaction tear sheet - Trading costs and turnover
Round trip tear sheet - Complete trade cycle analysis
Capacity tear sheet - Liquidity and scalability analysis
Performance attribution tear sheet - Factor-based return attribution
tear_sheets.key_insight: Tear sheets are the primary interface for pyfolio - they provide at-a-glance comprehensive portfolio analysis

returns_analysis.description: Analysis of strategy returns over time

volatility_metrics: Downside risk - Deviation below target return
drawdown_metrics: Drawdown periods - Duration and magnitude of declines
Underwater plots - Visualization of drawdown recovery
Top drawdown table - Worst drawdown events
tail_risk: Value at Risk (VaR) - Expected loss at confidence level
Tail ratio - Right tail to left tail comparison

metrics.name: Sharpe Ratio
metrics.interpretation: Higher is better, >1 is good, >2 is very good

metrics.name: Sortino Ratio
metrics.interpretation: Like Sharpe but only penalizes downside volatility

metrics.name: Calmar Ratio
metrics.interpretation: Return per unit of worst drawdown

metrics.name: Omega Ratio
metrics.interpretation: Ratio of gains to losses above/below threshold

metrics.name: Alpha
metrics.description: Excess return beyond what beta would predict

metrics.name: Beta
metrics.interpretation: Beta=1 moves with market, >1 more volatile, <1 less volatile

metrics: Gross leverage - Total exposure relative to capital
Net leverage - Long minus short exposure

metrics.name: Turnover
metrics.description: Trading activity relative to portfolio size
denominators: AGB (Actual Gross Book) - Sum of absolute position values

metrics.name: Daily Volume
metrics.description: Dollar volume of trades per day

metrics.name: Slippage
metrics.description: Execution price deviation from expected price

round_trip_analysis.description: Complete trade cycle analysis from entry to exit
round_trip_analysis.definition: Round trip starts when opening a position and completes when shares return to zero
metrics: PnL per round trip - Profit/loss for each complete trade
Duration - Time from entry to exit
Win rate - Percentage of profitable trades
Average winner vs loser - Comparative profitability
Long vs short performance - Directional comparison
visualizations: Round trip lifetime plots - Duration and direction

concepts.name: Days to Liquidate
concepts.description: Time required to exit positions given volume constraints
concepts.constraint: Typically limited to consuming small % of daily volume

concepts.name: Capacity Sweep
concepts.description: Projected performance at different capital levels
concepts.shows: How Sharpe ratio degrades with increased capital

concepts.name: Low Liquidity Transactions
concepts.description: Trades consuming high proportion of available volume

concepts.name: Slippage Penalty
concepts.description: Adjustment for price impact based on volume

performance_attribution.methodology: Attributes portfolio returns to systematic (factor) and specific (alpha) sources
inputs: Factor returns - Time series of factor performance
Factor loadings - Security exposures to each factor
visualizations: Total vs common vs specific returns

bayesian_analysis.key_benefit: Provides probability distributions instead of point estimates, showing confidence in metrics

features.name: Bayesian Cone
improvements_over_standard: Incorporates uncertainty (wider cone for short backtests)
Uses Student-T distribution (heavier tails than normal)

features.name: Mean Returns Comparison
features.description: In-sample vs out-of-sample comparison with uncertainty
features.shows: Posterior distributions of mean returns, not point estimates

features.name: Parameter Posteriors
features.description: Probability distributions for Sharpe, volatility, etc.

interesting_periods.description: Strategy behavior during significant market events
interesting_periods.purpose: Understand how strategy performs during stress periods
interesting_periods.usage: Plots strategy returns overlaid on benchmark during these periods

intraday_strategies.description: Special handling for strategies that close positions daily
intraday_strategies.detection: Automatic via estimate_intraday='infer' or manual override
intraday_strategies.processing: Resamples positions to peak exposure point (typically end-of-day)

positions.values: float - Dollar value or share count
positions.note: Can be absolute values or normalized

typical_workflows.name: Basic Analysis from Zipline
steps: Generate full tear sheet: pf.create_full_tear_sheet(returns, positions, transactions)

typical_workflows.name: Factor Attribution Analysis
steps: Obtain factor returns (e.g., from research platform)

typical_workflows.name: Capacity Analysis
steps: Identify liquidity constraints and scaling limits

typical_workflows.name: Custom Metric Calculation
steps: Use individual functions from timeseries module

practices: Ensure returns are simple returns, not log returns
Use consistent timezone (UTC recommended)
Remove any NaN or inf values from returns
Verify transactions have required columns (symbol, amount, price)
Include cash column in positions DataFrame
Always specify live_start_date to separate in-sample from out-of-sample
Include benchmark for proper context
Use sector_mappings for better position analysis
Run round trip analysis to understand trade efficiency
Check capacity constraints for real-world applicability
Start with create_simple_tear_sheet for quick overview
Use return_fig=True to save figures instead of displaying
Bootstrap analysis is slow - use sparingly
Bayesian analysis requires PyMC3 and is computationally intensive
Cache results for repeated analysis
Look at risk-adjusted metrics (Sharpe, Sortino) not just returns
Examine drawdown characteristics - recovery time matters
Check rolling metrics for stability over time
Compare in-sample vs out-of-sample performance
Verify results aren't driven by few large positions (check concentration)
Consider capacity constraints for live trading
Use interesting periods to test strategy in various market conditions

common_pitfalls.issue: Using log returns instead of simple returns

common_pitfalls.solution: Add cash column or ensure positions sum correctly
Convert all timestamps to UTC: df.index = df.index.tz_localize('UTC')
Always use live_start_date to separate OOS period
Include commissions in transactions, use slippage parameter
Run capacity tear sheet to verify strategy is tradeable at scale
Use appropriate benchmark for strategy type

zipline.description: Primary integration - Zipline is algorithmic trading library
zipline.workflow: Run Zipline backtest → Extract data → Generate tear sheets

quantopian.note: Originally designed for Quantopian platform (now defunct)

jupyter.description: Best used in Jupyter notebooks for interactive analysis
jupyter.features: HTML tables, inline plots, interactive exploration

requirements: Pass to appropriate tear sheet function

limitations_and_considerations.recommendation: Use pyfolio-reloaded for modern Python support

limitations_and_considerations.workaround: May need to resample higher-frequency data

limitations_and_considerations.limitation: Limited support for multi-asset class portfolios

limitations_and_considerations.consideration: May take minutes to hours for MCMC sampling

limitations_and_considerations.requirement: Need OHLCV data for all traded symbols

limitations_and_considerations.source: Need to obtain factor returns from research provider

alternatives_and_ecosystem.description: Common financial risk and performance metrics
Algorithmic trading library, pyfolio analyzes Zipline results
Similar portfolio analytics with HTML reports
Complete backtesting platform with PyFolio analyzer

common_patterns: Tear sheets use decorator pattern for consistent styling
Most functions accept optional ax parameter for subplot integration
return_fig parameter allows programmatic figure handling
Consistent parameter naming across modules (returns, positions, transactions)
Vectorized operations using pandas for performance
best_practices_code: Use extract_rets_pos_txn_from_zipline for standardized data extraction
Leverage utils.print_table for consistent output formatting
Use utils.check_intraday for automatic strategy type detection
Apply @customize decorator for consistent plot styling
Use cone_std parameter for customized confidence intervals
pitfalls_code: Don't modify returns in place - functions may cache
Watch for duplicate timestamps in transactions
Be careful with turnover_denom - AGB vs portfolio_value give different results
Bootstrap functions are not deterministic without random_seed

implementation_recommendations.rationale: Minimal data requirements, fast execution, covers key metrics

implementation_recommendations.scenario: Complete portfolio analysis with all data
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Comprehensive view including all aspects of performance
Detailed analysis of individual trades and patterns
Decompose returns into systematic and specific components
Identify liquidity constraints and capital limits

implementation_recommendations.recommended_solution: Use individual timeseries functions (sharpe_ratio, max_drawdown, etc.)
implementation_recommendations.rationale: Flexible composition of specific metrics needed
Provides probability distributions for metrics, shows confidence

popular_solutions: Combination with Zipline for end-to-end backtesting
Custom tear sheets by extending plotting module
Export to HTML/PDF for client reporting
controversial_topics: Choice of turnover denominator (AGB vs portfolio_value)
Appropriateness of Bayesian analysis for short time series
Treatment of transaction costs in returns
expert_opinions: Always separate in-sample from out-of-sample using live_start_date
Risk-adjusted metrics more important than absolute returns
Capacity analysis often overlooked but critical for real money
Round trip analysis reveals trading discipline issues
Rolling metrics show stability/regime changes better than cumulative
